summary: Finished matching the egg item module by switching itegg from assembly to C, deleting asm/melee/it/items/itegg.s, marking src/melee/it/items/itegg.c as Matching in configure.py, and updating obj_files.mk to build the C object. The C changes were small but match-critical: variable declaration/order tweaks in it_80288C88, an inline attrRand wrapper around HSD_Randi, direct user_data access in it_80289218, PAD_STACK size increases in several wrapper callbacks, and bool return types in itegg.h.
what_changed: Removed the 834-line hand-written/remaining assembly file asm/melee/it/items/itegg.s.
Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itegg.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Changed obj_files.mk so itegg is built from $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itegg.c.o instead of the asm object.
In it_80288C88, moved SpawnItem, Item*, and Item_GObj* declarations to function scope and renamed the return variable from ret to item_gobj, matching the needed codegen shape.
In it_80288C88, set ip->xDD0_flag.bits.b6 to false for the spawned egg item in the matching C.
Added inline s32 attrRand(EggVars* attrs) returning HSD_Randi(attrs->rand_max), then used it in it_80288DC4.
Changed it_80289218 to get the Item pointer via gobj->user_data instead of GET_ITEM(gobj).
Changed PAD_STACK from 4 to 8 in it_802893D4, it_80289470, it_8028950C, and it_802895A8.
Updated itegg.h prototypes for it_80289218, it_802893D4, it_80289470, it_8028950C, and it_802895A8 from int to bool.
smart_moves: Used build metadata changes as the final signal of completion: both configure.py and obj_files.mk were updated in the same PR that deleted the asm file.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack/codegen details with PAD_STACK(8) on thin wrapper functions that just return it_80289218(gobj).
Introduced a tiny inline attrRand wrapper around HSD_Randi(attrs->rand_max), likely to influence inlining/register allocation without obscuring behavior.
Aligned header prototypes with the C implementation by using bool for predicate-style item callbacks.
Used direct gobj->user_data access in it_80289218 where GET_ITEM did not produce the desired match.
decomp_lessons: For item modules, finishing a match usually requires three coordinated changes: remove the asm file, flip configure.py to Object(Matching, ...), and switch obj_files.mk from asm .s.o to src .c.o.
Seemingly cosmetic local variable placement and naming can matter for matching; in it_80288C88, declarations were hoisted and the return variable was reshaped.
Small inline wrappers can be a useful codegen tactic around library calls such as HSD_Randi when direct calls do not match.
Wrapper callbacks that forward to another predicate may still need explicit PAD_STACK values to preserve the original stack frame.
Header return types should reflect bool semantics when the implementation and assembly behave as boolean predicates.
naming_conventions: The file keeps address-based symbol names such as it_80288C88, it_80288DC4, it_80289218, and it_803F5988.
The helper added in C uses descriptive lower camel case, attrRand, for a local attribute randomization wrapper.
The return object local in it_80288C88 was renamed from generic ret to item_gobj, matching the Item_GObj domain type.
Egg item special attributes are referred to as EggVars* attrs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted asm/melee/it/items/itegg.s after the C version matched.
Switched the object list from asm/melee/it/items/itegg.s.o to src/melee/it/items/itegg.c.o.
Marked the C file as Matching rather than NonMatching in configure.py.
Used PAD_STACK(8) instead of PAD_STACK(4) in several simple bool-returning wrappers.
Used an inline attrRand helper to wrap HSD_Randi(attrs->rand_max).
Used gobj->user_data directly instead of GET_ITEM(gobj) in it_80289218.
Hoisted local declarations in it_80288C88 to adjust generated code.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that casts such as GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) gobj) are unnecessary; m2c generates them unless run with --no-casts. Suggested using Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj);

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the casts were copied from decomp.me and said they would be resolved in a follow-up.

follow_up_queries: Find follow-up PRs removing unnecessary GET_ITEM HSD_GObj casts copied from decomp.me.
Search for other item modules where PAD_STACK size changes were required for simple forwarding callbacks.
Compare other item match PRs that use direct gobj->user_data access instead of GET_ITEM.
Search for inline wrappers around HSD_Randi used as matching tactics.
